info.title: WOS Public API
info.description: ADR 0082 public REST surface â€” emitted from `#[utoipa::path]` annotations on `wos-server` handlers and `utoipa::ToSchema` derived types under `crate::api::types`.

get.summary:
`GET /api/v1/applicant/cases` â€” list the calling applicant's cases.
Cursor-paginated per ADR 0082 D-7.

parameters.name: lifecycleState
parameters.description: Filter to a specific lifecycle posture.

parameters.name: actionNeeded
parameters.description: Filter to cases with at least one applicant-owed pending/drafted task.

get.summary:
`GET /api/v1/applicant/cases/{id}` â€” full applicant-visible
projection. Returns `WOS-1404` when the case is not visible.

404.description: Case URN not visible to the calling applicant.

get.summary:
`GET /api/v1/applicant/cases/{id}/tasks` â€” applicant-owed task
subresource. Cursor-paginated.

404.description: Case URN not visible to the calling applicant.

get.summary:
`GET /api/v1/applicant/notifications` â€” applicant inbox feed.
Cursor-paginated.

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/audit/queries` â€” submit a cross-case audit query.
Synchronous when `materialize: false` (default); deferred polling
when `materialize: true` per the audit spec.

200.description: Synchronous query result page or accepted materialized handle.

422.description: Request body fails schema validation (e.g., empty `tenant`, inverted `timeRange`).

get.summary:
`GET /api/v1/audit/queries/{queryId}` â€” fetch a materialized query's
status + first page. Returns `WOS-1404` for unknown ids.

200.description: Query status plus first result page.

404.description: Query id not visible to caller.

get.summary:
`GET /api/v1/audit/queries/{queryId}/results?cursor=...&limit=...` â€”
cursor-paginate a materialized result set per ADR 0082 D-7.

404.description: Query id not visible to caller.

get.summary:
`GET /api/v1/audit/records/{provenanceUrn}` â€” fetch a single
provenance record by URN under audit scope. Same shape as the
per-case provenance endpoint.

404.description: Record URN not visible to caller.

get.summary:
`GET /api/v1/auth/introspect` â€” RFC 7662-style. Returns `active: true`
plus scope/assurance for a valid bearer; `active: false` when the
token does not authenticate.

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/auth/login` â€” issue a bearer + refresh token. Per ADR 0082
D-16 `Idempotency-Key` is REQUIRED; the route layer enforces it.

200.description: Bearer + refresh token issued (or MFA challenge pending).

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/auth/logout` â€” revoke the bearer's session (this-session)
or every active session for the principal (all-sessions).

`POST /api/v1/auth/refresh` â€” redeem a refresh token for a fresh access
token. Refresh does NOT swap scope (use scope-swap).

200.description: Fresh access token (and rotated refresh token).

401.description: Refresh token expired or already redeemed.

get.summary:
`GET /api/v1/auth/scope` â€” current scope context for the bearer's
session. Anonymous request â†’ 401.

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/auth/scope-swap` â€” swap the session's scope within the
current tenant. Cross-tenant swaps rejected per ADR 0068 D-1 (Proposed).
`Idempotency-Key` REQUIRED per ADR 0082 D-16.

200.description: Fresh bearer token bound to the new scope.

403.description: Cross-tenant or unauthorized scope swap rejected.

post.summary: Directly append an event to a durable case ledger.

parameters.name: case_id
parameters.description: Durable case-ledger TypeID or WOS resource URN

400.description: Invalid event contract or idempotency token

403.description: Caller lacks permission for the event branch

409.description: Case ledger already exists or append sequence conflicts

503.description: Post-ledger append persistence failed after authorization

get.summary:
`GET /api/v1/correspondence` â€” cursor-paginated list. Default ordering
per spec is `occurredAt` descending.

parameters.name: processId
parameters.description: Filter to a specific workflow process URN.

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/correspondence` â€” log a correspondence message.
Idempotency-Key REQUIRED (ADR 0082 D-16). The route layer enforces it.

get.summary:
`GET /api/v1/correspondence/calendar/{calendarId}/dates?from=...&to=...`.
Read-only date series projection of the delivery sidecar's calendar block.

parameters.name: calendarId
parameters.description: Calendar identifier from the delivery sidecar.

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/correspondence/renderings` â€” render a delivery template.
Subsumes the legacy `POST /notifications/{workflowUrl}/render` (PLN-0402).
Idempotency-Key REQUIRED.

400.description: Idempotency-Key missing or workflow has no notification-template sidecar.

404.description: Workflow URI or templateRef not visible.

get.summary:
`GET /api/v1/correspondence/renderings/{id}` â€” read a previously
produced rendering. Renderings are immutable.
`GET /api/v1/correspondence/{id}` â€” read one correspondence message.

`GET /api/v1/dashboard/lifecycle-rollup` â€” closed-map case counts
keyed by `LifecycleState`.

`GET /api/v1/dashboard/recent-activity` â€” counts grouped by
`RecentActivityEventClass`.

`GET /api/v1/dashboard/sla-breaches` â€” count + earliest/latest
breach envelope.

`GET /api/v1/dashboard/summary` â€” composed dashboard rollup
(lifecycle + SLA breaches + recent activity) under a single
envelope-level `asOf`.
`GET /api/v1/reports/runs` â€” cursor-paginated list of runs.

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/reports/runs` â€” queue a new report run.
Idempotency-Key REQUIRED (ADR 0082 D-16).

422.description: Input fails schema validation (vendor-extension reportType, inverted timeRange, max-duration out of range).

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/reports/runs/{id}` â€” read a run envelope.

`GET /api/v1/reports/runs/{id}/export?format=csv|json|parquet` â€” stream
the result body in the requested closed format. Default is `json`.

`GET /api/v1/reports/runs/{id}/rows` â€” cursor-paginated typed rows.
Available only after `status == succeeded`; otherwise responds 409.

ActorRef.description:
Stable actor reference from the identity/governance subsystem (ADR 0082 D-9). The principal-class segment is the closed VISION Â§V taxonomy `human | service-account | workload | support`. Identity details live once in the identity/governance subsystem; every record references actors by URN. Replaces the legacy nested `actor: {id,type,name}` shape from prior portal art.

 <details><summary>JSON schema</summary>

 ```json
{
  "description": "Stable actor reference from the identity/governance subsystem (ADR 0082 D-9). The principal-class segment is the closed VISION Â§V taxonomy `human | service-account | workload | support`. Identity details live once in the identity/governance subsystem; every record references actors by URN. Replaces the legacy nested `actor: {id,type,name}` shape from prior portal art.",
  "examples": [
    "actor:human:caseworker-17",
    "actor:service-account:notice-mailer",
    "actor:workload:wos-runtime",
    "actor:support:agency-admin-42"
  ],
  "type": "string",
  "pattern": "^actor:(human|service-account|workload|support):[A-Za-z0-9._-]+$"
}
 ```
 </details>

environment.description: Closed deployment environment (VISION Â§V). `sandbox` = dev/test; `staging` = pre-prod; `prod` = live. New environments require a major schema bump.

organization.description: Organization identifier â€” product-ownership boundary where forms, cases, and members live (VISION Â§V; ADR 0068 D-1 (Proposed)). Object-ownership and deletion-follows-org invariants tie retention to this id, not to billing state.

tenant.description: Tenant identifier â€” outermost hosting/commercial boundary (VISION Â§V; ADR 0068 D-1 (Proposed)). Hosting-tier identity, not user-visible.

oneOf.description: Optional workspace identifier â€” sub-scope inside an organization (VISION Â§V). Omitted when the actor is bound at the organization level.

ActorScopeEnvironment.description:
Closed deployment environment (VISION Â§V). `sandbox` = dev/test; `staging` = pre-prod; `prod` = live. New environments require a major schema bump.

 <details><summary>JSON schema</summary>

 ```json
{
  "description": "Closed deployment environment (VISION Â§V). `sandbox` = dev/test; `staging` = pre-prod; `prod` = live. New environments require a major schema bump.",
  "enum": [
    "sandbox",
    "staging",
    "prod"
  ]
}
 ```
 </details>

ActorScopeTenant.description:
Tenant identifier â€” outermost hosting/commercial boundary (VISION Â§V; ADR 0068 D-1 (Proposed)). Hosting-tier identity, not user-visible.

 <details><summary>JSON schema</summary>

 ```json
{
  "description": "Tenant identifier â€” outermost hosting/commercial boundary (VISION Â§V; ADR 0068 D-1 (Proposed)). Hosting-tier identity, not user-visible.",
  "examples": [
    "tenant-acme",
    "tenant-formspec-public"
  ],
  "type": "string",
  "minLength": 1,
  "x-wos": {
    "openStringKind": "identifier"
  }
}
 ```
 </details>

oneOf.description: Optional agent URN to restrict the report to a single shadow agent.

reportType.description: Discriminator literal selecting the agent-shadow-mode-divergence input variant.

timeRange.description: UTC time window over which divergence comparisons are aggregated.

oneOf.description: Author-time workflow URL to scope the report to. Omit to span all visible workflows.

agentId.description: Agent URN this row is attributed to.

averageDivergenceMagnitude.description: Mean normalized disagreement magnitude across diverging comparisons. Method-specific units; consult the workflow's shadow-mode declaration.

oneOf.description: Identifier of the baseline the shadow agent was compared against (`human` for human-reviewer baseline, otherwise a model identifier). Open identifier â€” projected from the agent's `shadowMode.baselineModelIdentifier`.

comparisonsCount.description: Number of shadow-vs-baseline comparisons observed within the window.

divergenceCount.description: Number of comparisons whose normalized disagreement exceeded the agent's `shadowMode.divergenceThreshold`.

divergenceRate.description: Divergence rate in [0,1] for this agent within the window â€” `divergenceCount / comparisonsCount`.

reportType.description: Discriminator literal selecting the agent-shadow-mode-divergence row variant.

AggregationGranularity.description:
Closed time-bucket granularity for time-series report inputs. Bucket boundaries are aligned to UTC calendar units.

 <details><summary>JSON schema</summary>

 ```json
{
  "description": "Closed time-bucket granularity for time-series report inputs. Bucket boundaries are aligned to UTC calendar units.",
  "examples": [
    "month"
  ],
  "enum": [
    "day",
    "week",
    "month",
    "quarter"
  ]
}
 ```
 </details>

oneOf.description: Optional agent URN to restrict the report to a single declared agent.

reportType.description: Discriminator literal selecting the AI override-rate input variant.

timeRange.description: UTC time window over which override counts are aggregated.

oneOf.description: Author-time workflow URL to scope the report to. Omit to span all visible workflows.

agentId.description: Agent URN this row is attributed to.

decisionsCount.description: Number of agent decisions observed within the window.

overrideCount.description: Number of reviewer overrides recorded for this agent within the window.

overrideRate.description: Reviewer override rate in [0,1] for this agent.

reportType.description: Discriminator literal selecting the AI override-rate row variant.

confidence.description: Reported calibrated confidence of the agent's contribution to this case in [0,1]. Optional â€” surfaced only when the agent reported a confidence and disclosure policy permits showing it; absent otherwise.

displayName.description: Plain-language agent label suitable for applicant rendering ("Eligibility Assistant", not `claude-sonnet-4-20250514`). Open prose â€” the staff `AgentView.modelIdentifier` is intentionally NOT projected here.

roleInDecision.description: Closed-no-extension role classification matching the disclosure obligation. `advisory` is suggest-only or read-only assistance; `primary` is the agent that produced the determination input (autonomous or supervised); `fallback` is an agent that ran only when an upstream actor failed. The applicant has authority to know which of the three roles the AI played â€” closed by design so the disclosure cannot drift into vendor-specific labels.

ApplicantAgentSummaryRoleInDecision.description:
Closed-no-extension role classification matching the disclosure obligation. `advisory` is suggest-only or read-only assistance; `primary` is the agent that produced the determination input (autonomous or supervised); `fallback` is an agent that ran only when an upstream actor failed. The applicant has authority to know which of the three roles the AI played â€” closed by design so the disclosure cannot drift into vendor-specific labels.

 <details><summary>JSON schema</summary>

 ```json
{
  "description": "Closed-no-extension role classification matching the disclosure obligation. `advisory` is suggest-only or read-only assistance; `primary` is the agent that produced the determination input (autonomous or supervised); `fallback` is an agent that ran only when an upstream actor failed. The applicant has authority to know which of the three roles the AI played â€” closed by design so the disclosure cannot drift into vendor-specific labels.",
  "examples": [
    "advisory"
  ],
  "enum": [
    "advisory",
    "primary",
    "fallback"
  ]
}
 ```
 </details>

agentsInvolved.description: Agents whose actions contributed to this case, ordered by `roleInDecision` (`primary` first, then `advisory`, then `fallback`). Empty when no agent participated despite the workflow declaring AI capability â€” disclosure on a per-case-actual-involvement basis is the regulatory obligation.

humanReviewedAllAgentDecisions.description: True when every agent action on this case was followed by recorded human review per the workflow's review-protocol obligation. False signals that at least one agent action took effect without per-action human review (`autonomous` autonomy with after-the-fact review or sample-based review). Load-bearing for the applicant's ability to assess appeal grounds and for the staff's confidence in the determination chain.

narrativeRecordCount.description: Count of narrative-tier provenance records on this case (ai-integration.md Â§13). The applicant uses this as a hint for further inquiry â€” "there are 3 narrative records about this case" â€” without exposing the record contents themselves at this projection.

oneOf.description: EU AI Act Art. 13 / OMB M-24-10 disclosure of AI involvement on this case (ai-integration.md Â§12.1-Â§12.2 line 592-604). Present iff the governing workflow's `aiOversight.disclosure.discloseThatAgentAssisted` is true. Exposes the FACT of AI involvement and the operational shape (agent count, role, human-review coverage, narrative-record count) â€” never internal model identifiers or autonomy enums. Closes the structural disclosure gap on the applicant-facing surface.

openTasks.description: Applicant-owed tasks currently in `pending` or `drafted` status, ordered by `deadline` ascending then `createdAt` ascending. Bounded set per case; the dedicated subresource `GET /api/v1/applicant/cases/{id}/tasks` is the cursor-paginated form for cases with many historical tasks.

recentNotifications.description: Most recent applicant notifications scoped to this case, ordered by `createdAt` descending. Bounded by server-side cap (typically the most recent 20). Full feed at `GET /api/v1/applicant/notifications`.

statusTimeline.description: Applicant-visible status timeline for this case, ordered by `occurredAt` ascending. Bounded set per case (governed events the applicant has authority to see).

summary.description: Case summary envelope â€” same shape as the list-view item, repeated for self-containment.

actionNeeded.description: True when at least one applicant-owed task on this case is currently in `pending` or `drafted` status (i.e. the applicant has work outstanding). Computed server-side.

continuationOfServicesActive.description: True when the case is operating under a continuation-of-services policy (governance Â§3.6 â€” `AppealMechanism.continuationOfServices`; workflow-governance.md:154-158). Surfaces the existence of the policy without leaking the underlying rationale; the applicant has unambiguous authority to see this fact because it directly bears on whether benefits continue during appeal.

oneOf.description: Absolute UTC RFC 3339 timestamp at which the active continuation-of-services window closes, or the literal `never` for indefinite continuation (governance Â§3.6; ADR 0082 D-10 sentinel pattern). Present only when `continuationOfServicesActive == true`.

createdAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the case was created.

id.description: Stable public URN for this workflow process.

lifecycleState.description: Closed lifecycle posture for this case (projected from `instance.schema.json`).

oneOf.description: Short human-readable case label suitable for list rendering. Sourced from the workflow's case-file `caseLabel` mapping when configured; falls back to a workflow-name-based default. Open prose â€” rendering concern.

updatedAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp of the most recent applicant-visible update.

workflowUrl.description: Absolute URI of the governing Workflow Document.

oneOf.description: Opaque token for the next page. Omitted when hasMore is false.

hasMore.description: True when another page is available from the server.

items.description: Applicant case summaries in this page.

body.description: Notification body text suitable for in-app display.

createdAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the notification was created.

id.description: Stable public URN for this notification.

kind.description: Closed-with-vendor-extension applicant-facing notification family (ADR 0082 D-12). Reserved literals are the applicant-relevant projection of staff event families: `task-assigned` (new applicant-owed task); `task-deadline-approaching` (advance SLA warning); `case-update` (lifecycle posture change visible to the applicant); `correspondence-received` (inbound message addressed to the applicant); `decision-reached` (rights-impacting determination available). Staff-internal categories (`escalation`, `review-activated`, `hold-expired`) are filtered server-side and MUST NOT appear here. Vendor extensions MUST use an `^x-[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$` prefix.

oneOf.description: Workflow process URN this notification relates to. Omitted only for tenant-wide system notifications.

readAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the applicant marked the notification read. Omitted while `status == unread`.

status.description: Closed applicant-side read status. Closed-no-extension by design â€” the applicant inbox lifecycle is normative. Note this is a strict subset of `notification.schema.json#/$defs/NotificationStatus`: the staff `dismissed` status is not exposed because applicant notifications are never dismissed administratively, only read or archived by the applicant.

oneOf.description: Task URN this notification relates to. Present iff `kind` is `task-assigned` or `task-deadline-approaching`.

title.description: Short headline suitable for inbox-style display.

ApplicantNotificationListItemStatus.description:
Closed applicant-side read status. Closed-no-extension by design â€” the applicant inbox lifecycle is normative. Note this is a strict subset of `notification.schema.json#/$defs/NotificationStatus`: the staff `dismissed` status is not exposed because applicant notifications are never dismissed administratively, only read or archived by the applicant.

 <details><summary>JSON schema</summary>

 ```json
{
  "description": "Closed applicant-side read status. Closed-no-extension by design â€” the applicant inbox lifecycle is normative. Note this is a strict subset of `notification.schema.json#/$defs/NotificationStatus`: the staff `dismissed` status is not exposed because applicant notifications are never dismissed administratively, only read or archived by the applicant.",
  "examples": [
    "unread"
  ],
  "enum": [
    "unread",
    "read",
    "archived"
  ]
}
 ```
 </details>

oneOf.description: Opaque token for the next page. Omitted when hasMore is false.

hasMore.description: True when another page is available from the server.

event.description: Closed-with-vendor-extension applicant-visible event class. Reserved literals project the events the applicant has authority to see; staff-internal events (delegation, review-flagged, hold-engaged rationale) are filtered server-side. Vendor extensions MUST use an `^x-[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$` prefix.

oneOf.description: New lifecycle posture entered as a result of this event. Present iff `event == lifecycle-changed`.

occurredAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the event was recorded server-side.

oneOf.description: Short human-readable summary of the event suitable for timeline rendering. Open prose â€” rendering concern.
Task URN this entry relates to. Present iff `event` is `applicant-task-assigned` or `applicant-task-submitted`.
Opaque token for the next page. Omitted when hasMore is false.

hasMore.description: True when another page is available from the server.

items.description: Applicant task summaries in this page.

createdAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the task was created.

deadline.description: UTC RFC 3339 deadline by which the applicant SHOULD submit the response before SLA breach (governance S10.3).

id.description: Stable public URN for this task.

kind.description: Closed-with-vendor-extension task family for applicant-owed work (ADR 0082 D-12). Reserved literals are the applicant-actionable subset of `task.schema.json#/$defs/TaskKind`: `intake` (initial application capture); `correspondence-response` (reply to inbound correspondence); `signature` (signature-profile signer step); `verification` (independent confirmation of a prior mutation). Staff-owned families (`review`, `determination`, `escalation`, `hold-resolution`) are filtered server-side and MUST NOT appear here. Vendor extensions MUST use an `^x-[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$` prefix.

processId.description: Workflow process URN this task belongs to.

status.description: Closed user-facing task posture, mirroring `task.schema.json#/$defs/TaskStatus` (ADR 0082 D-12). Reserved literals `pending | drafted | submitted | dismissed | expired`. The `pattern` branch is a forward-compatibility seam only â€” `task.schema.json#/$defs/TaskStatus` itself is closed-no-extension; vendor extensions are intentionally not exercised here.

title.description: Short human-readable task headline (open prose; rendering concern, not contract).

updatedAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the task projection was last refreshed.

oneOf.description: Optional human-readable detail explaining the gate result. Free-form prose for cases where the structured evidence does not carry the full story.

evidenceRefs.description: Structured evidence references supporting the gate's pass/fail finding (governance Â§5.4 â€” "each gate produces a pass/fail result with structured evidence"). Empty when the gate verification did not surface specific evidence references (typical for `format` parse checks); populated for `source-grounded`, `cross-document`, and `consistency` gates whose evidence is referenceable.

gateKind.description: Closed-with-vendor-extension classification of which Â§5.4 gate produced this result.

gatePassed.description: True when the gate's verification method (governance Â§5.4 third column) returned pass; false when it returned fail. The pipeline's risk profile is determined by the weakest gate (workflow-governance.md:309), so any false here load-bears.

oneOf.description: Optional pipeline-stage identifier this gate evaluation belongs to. Useful when multiple gates fire in one stage and consumers want to correlate by stage.

attestations.description: The chain of identity-attestation records observed for this subject within the queried scope, ordered by `attestedAt` ascending. Each entry is a Facts-tier `ProvenanceRecord` whose `recordKind == identityAttestation` (cross-`$ref`ed; the audit projection is the per-case shape).

earliestAttestedAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp of the earliest attestation in the chain. Omitted when the chain is empty.

oneOf.description: Highest assurance level observed across the attestation chain (closed taxonomy from ADR 0068 D-3.1 + vendor-prefix extension per ADR 0082 D-12). Mirrors `wos-workflow.schema.json` `IdentityAttestationRecord.assuranceLevel`. Computed by the server from the chain; useful for compliance investigators answering 'did this subject ever reach high assurance under this provider?'.

latestAttestedAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp of the most-recent attestation in the chain. Omitted when the chain is empty.

subjectGlobalId.description: Tenant-independent subject identifier (URI; e.g., `did:web:...`, `urn:idp:subject:...`) per ADR 0068 D-3.1. The view aggregates every `IdentityAttestation` record naming this subject across visible cases.

oneOf.description: Optional actor URN filter â€” restrict the query to records whose `actorRef` equals this principal (ADR 0082 D-9). Identity-attestation investigations use this filter against a specific subject.

instanceScope.description: Optional instance scope â€” restrict the result set to records belonging to one or more named workflow processes. Empty array is forbidden. When omitted the query spans every visible instance in scope.

materialize.description: When true, the server materializes the result set so it can be paginated across multiple GET requests. When false (default), the query executes synchronously and `POST /api/v1/audit/queries` returns the first page directly. `Idempotency-Key` is REQUIRED when `materialize: true` (ADR 0082 D-16) because materialization is an externally-visible side effect.

maxResults.description: Hard upper bound on the result set, applied after every other filter. Servers MAY enforce a smaller per-deployment ceiling. The page size is governed by `limit` on the result endpoints; this bound caps the total result corpus the query materializes.

recordKindFilter.description: Optional record-kind filter â€” restrict the result set to one or more Facts-tier `recordKind` literals (e.g., `identityAttestation`, `signatureAffirmation`, `caseStateMutation`). Cross-`$ref`s the closed-with-vendor-extension `FactsRecordKind` taxonomy from `provenance.schema.json`.

scope.description: Tenant / Organization / Workspace / Environment scope (ADR 0068 D-2 Proposed). Tenant is REQUIRED; cross-tenant queries are refused at the runtime.

oneOf.description: Result-set ordering. Defaults to `timestamp-desc` when omitted.

tierFilter.description: Optional tier filter â€” restrict the result set to one or more provenance tiers. Empty array is forbidden; omit the field to span all four tiers.

end.description: Inclusive UTC RFC 3339 upper bound for the audit window.

start.description: Inclusive UTC RFC 3339 lower bound for the audit window.

completedAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the query reached its terminal status. Omitted while `status in {pending, running}`.

oneOf.description: Opaque token for the next page. Omitted when hasMore is false.

hasMore.description: True when another page is available from the server.

items.description: Projected provenance records in this page. Each item is a full tier-discriminated `ProvenanceRecord` from `provenance.schema.json` â€” no separate `audit-record` type is invented (audit is cross-case provenance, not a different shape).

queryId.description: Server-issued opaque identifier for the materialized query, used to fetch subsequent pages and to cancel. Empty / synchronous queries (when `materialize: false`) MAY return a stable identifier the client can ignore.

status.description: Lifecycle status of the underlying materialized query. Synchronous queries (when `materialize: false`) return `succeeded` directly.

submittedAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the query was accepted by the server. Useful for reproducibility â€” a query submitted at instant T against a tenant with active appends MAY return different rows than one submitted at T+1.

oneOf.description: Optional longer human-readable failure description.

wosErrorCode.description: Stable WOS error code from the public error registry that classifies this failure.

AuditQueryResultFailureWosErrorCode.description:
Stable WOS error code from the public error registry that classifies this failure.

 <details><summary>JSON schema</summary>

 ```json
{
  "description": "Stable WOS error code from the public error registry that classifies this failure.",
  "type": "string",
  "pattern": "^WOS-[0-9]{4,5}$"
}
 ```
 </details>

AuditQuerySort.description:
Closed sort taxonomy for cross-case audit results. `timestamp-asc` = oldest record first (chronological investigations); `timestamp-desc` = newest first (recent-activity reviews). Closed enum with no extension seam â€” sort orders are normative and a verifier MUST be able to reproduce a result set deterministically.

 <details><summary>JSON schema</summary>

 ```json
{
  "description": "Closed sort taxonomy for cross-case audit results. `timestamp-asc` = oldest record first (chronological investigations); `timestamp-desc` = newest first (recent-activity reviews). Closed enum with no extension seam â€” sort orders are normative and a verifier MUST be able to reproduce a result set deterministically.",
  "examples": [
    "timestamp-desc"
  ],
  "enum": [
    "timestamp-asc",
    "timestamp-desc"
  ]
}
 ```
 </details>

AuditQueryStatus.description:
Closed lifecycle status for a deferred audit query. `pending` = queued, not yet started; `running` = executing; `succeeded` = first page available; `failed` = terminated with error; `cancelled` = caller cancelled before completion.

 <details><summary>JSON schema</summary>

 ```json
{

Reports can scan up to `maxResults: 100000` rows, so a stuck runner must not stay `running` indefinitely; this ceiling bounds wall-clock execution. The server enforces a default of 3600 seconds when omitted; callers MAY raise it up to 86400 seconds (24 hours) -- the server applies a per-deployment ceiling and clamps higher requests downward without rejection. The resolved value is server-stamped on the resulting `ReportRun.maxDurationSeconds` so callers can see what was applied. LifecycleState.description:
Closed instance lifecycle posture (ADR 0082 D-12). Byte-aligned mirror of the kernel `InstanceStatus` enum at `wos-workflow.schema.json#/$defs/InstanceStatus` â€” itself the name-promoted alias of the inline `status` field on `wos-process.schema.json` (ADR 0082 D-13 gate 6 enforces this byte-parity). Closed-no-extension by design: `active` accepts events normally; `suspended` is paused; `migrating` is mid-definition-version-change; `completed` reached a top-level final state; `terminated` was explicitly terminated; `stalled` exhausted the commit retry budget (ADR 0070 D-5). The kernel taxonomy is closed; if a deployment needs new lifecycle postures, the kernel grows them and the API mirror tracks.

 <details><summary>JSON schema</summary>

 ```json
{
  "description": "Closed instance lifecycle posture (ADR 0082 D-12). Byte-aligned mirror of the kernel `InstanceStatus` enum at `wos-workflow.schema.json#/$defs/InstanceStatus` â€” itself the name-promoted alias of the inline `status` field on `wos-process.schema.json` (ADR 0082 D-13 gate 6 enforces this byte-parity). Closed-no-extension by design: `active` accepts events normally; `suspended` is paused; `migrating` is mid-definition-version-change; `completed` reached a top-level final state; `terminated` was explicitly terminated; `stalled` exhausted the commit retry budget (ADR 0070 D-5). The kernel taxonomy is closed; if a deployment needs new lifecycle postures, the kernel grows them and the API mirror tracks.",
  "examples": [
    "active",
    "completed",
    "stalled"
  ],
  "enum": [
    "active",
    "suspended",
    "migrating",
    "completed",
    "terminated",
    "stalled"
  ],
  "x-wos": {
    "mirror": "wos-workflow.schema.json#/$defs/InstanceStatus"
  }
}
 ```
 </details>

RecentActivityEventClass.description:
Closed-with-vendor-extension event-class taxonomy (ADR 0082 D-12) for `RecentActivitySummary`. Reserved literals project the WOS event families a dashboard consumer typically wants to count: case-lifecycle (`case-created | case-completed | case-terminated`), task-lifecycle (`task-created | task-submitted | task-dismissed | task-expired`), review-protocol (`review-cleared | review-flagged`; governance S4), holds (`hold-engaged | hold-released`; governance S12), delegation (`delegation-granted | delegation-revoked`; governance S11), correspondence (`correspondence-sent | correspondence-received`). Vendor extensions MUST use an `^x-[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$` prefix â€” vendor-specific event classes do not break compatibility.

 <details><summary>JSON schema</summary>

 ```json
{
  "description": "Closed-with-vendor-extension event-class taxonomy (ADR 0082 D-12) for `RecentActivitySummary`. Reserved literals project the WOS event families a dashboard consumer typically wants to count: case-lifecycle (`case-created | case-completed | case-terminated`), task-lifecycle (`task-created | task-submitted | task-dismissed | task-expired`), review-protocol (`review-cleared | review-flagged`; governance S4), holds (`hold-engaged | hold-released`; governance S12), delegation (`delegation-granted | delegation-revoked`; governance S11), correspondence (`correspondence-sent | correspondence-received`). Vendor extensions MUST use an `^x-[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$` prefix â€” vendor-specific event classes do not break compatibility.",
  "examples": [
    "case-created",
    "task-submitted"
  ],
  "oneOf": [
    {
      "enum": [
        "case-created",
        "case-completed",
        "case-terminated",
        "task-created",
        "task-submitted",
        "task-dismissed",
        "task-expired",
        "review-cleared",
        "review-flagged",
        "hold-engaged",
        "hold-released",
        "delegation-granted",
        "delegation-revoked",
        "correspondence-sent",
        "correspondence-received"
      ]
    },
    {
      "type": "string",
      "pattern": "^x-[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$"
    }
  ]
}
 ```
 </details>

count.description: Number of events of this class observed in the requested range, computed as of `RecentActivitySummary.asOf`. Best-effort per ADR 0082 D-7.

WosResourceUrn.description:
Public WOS API resource URN (ADR 0092 D-1). Shape `urn:wos:<typeid>` â€” 3-segment URN whose namespace-specific string IS the TypeID. Strip `urn:wos:` to extract the canonical TypeID. The TypeID prefix carries the record family (`case | process | prov | gov | ai | assurance`); `case` identifies the durable case ledger, while `process` identifies a workflow runtime process bound to that ledger. Sub-resources (tasks, delegations, holds) are identified by URL path context, not independent TypeIDs. Task sub-resources append `/tasks/{taskId}` to the parent process URN.

 <details><summary>JSON schema</summary>

 ```json
{
  "description": "Public WOS API resource URN (ADR 0092 D-1). Shape `urn:wos:<typeid>` â€” 3-segment URN whose namespace-specific string IS the TypeID. Strip `urn:wos:` to extract the canonical TypeID. The TypeID prefix carries the record family (`case | process | prov | gov | ai | assurance`); `case` identifies the durable case ledger, while `process` identifies a workflow runtime process bound to that ledger. Sub-resources (tasks, delegations, holds) are identified by URL path context, not independent TypeIDs. Task sub-resources append `/tasks/{taskId}` to the parent process URN.",
  "examples": [
    "urn:wos:sba-poc_case_01jqrpd32jf8xtx9qxkkv3rqsc",
    "urn:wos:default_case_01hw7rm71vfay8vvw14d2pf2db",
    "urn:wos:default_process_01hw7rm71vfay8vvw14d2pf2db/tasks/complete-intake",
    "urn:wos:agency-gov_prov_01jqrxabcd3f8xtx9qxkkv3raa",
    "urn:wos:acme-corp_ai_01jqrpd32jf8xtx9qxkkv3rqsc"
  ],
  "type": "string",
  "pattern": "^urn:wos:[a-z][a-z0-9-]*_(case|process|prov|gov|ai|assurance|x-[a-z]+-[a-z]+)_[0-9a-hjkmnp-tv-z]{26}(?:/tasks/[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+)?$"
}
 ```
 </details>
